formatted_address: 14132 Trenton Ave, Orland Park, IL 60462, USA
pitch_rationale: The Orland Park, IL suburban context (northern climate, 1970s–1990s tract development) strongly suggests a mid-range pitch. Ground shadow lengths cast northward from the eaves measure approximately 6–9 feet at the image scale (1 px = 0.732 ft, calibrated to the 50-ft scale bar), consistent with a 5:12 rise-to-run for typical 8–9 ft eave heights. The roof-to-eave geometry shows ridgelines that are noticeably inset from the eave perimeter but not dramatically so, ruling out very shallow (3:12–4:12) or steep (8:12+) profiles. Hip-roof construction with asphalt shingles is apparent across multiple homes in the neighborhood, further consistent with a standard 5:12 suburban build. Moderate confidence reflects the lack of oblique imagery to directly measure vertical rise.
footprint_rationale: Subject home is a single-story ranch with a full hip roof (no gable ends, rake = 0). The plan is L-shaped: a main rectangular body (~280 px E-W × 205 px N-S = ~51 × 38 ft) plus a NE-projecting section (~185 px × 140 px = ~34 × 26 ft), likely an attached garage. Eave polygon traced at 8 vertices using the orange bounding box as reference (box spans ~347 × 275 px = 63.5 × 50.3 ft). Shoelace formula on the pixel polygon yields ~73,100 sq px × 0.033504 = 2,449 sq ft (~77% of bounding box, consistent with L-shape hip fill). Eaves summed around the full exterior perimeter (~222 ft). Ridges estimated from visible centerline peaks: ~15 ft main body + ~10 ft NE section. Hip lines estimated from 45° plan geometry: 4 corners on main body (~26.5 ft each) + 2 exposed corners on NE section (~18 ft each) = ~106 + 36 ft, rounded to 105 ft total. Valley where NE section meets main body estimated at ~26 ft. White vehicle visible at north end is parked in driveway and excluded. Confidence is moderate due to tree shadow and oblique view partially obscuring some eave corners.
fence_reason: vision roof area 2653 differs from solar 3170 by 16.3% (>12%)
